codeReview.summary: Fail: the reviewed feature wires up the requested Keychain, LocalAuthentication, and Info.plist pieces in `RCTNativeBiometricVault`, but it still misses two blocking requirements. The new Obj-C++ implementation is never added to the Xcode target, so the module can be absent from the app binary at runtime, and the error mapping still turns `errSecAuthFailed` into `AUTH_FAILED` even though a `kSecAccessControlBiometryCurrentSet` item can surface enrollment invalidation through that status, which should route to `KEY_INVALIDATED`.

issues.description: The feature description explicitly required `ios/EnboxMobile/NativeBiometricVault/RCTNativeBiometricVault.h` and `.mm` to be added to the Xcode target, but `PBXSourcesBuildPhase` still contains only `AppDelegate.swift in Sources` at lines 242-249 and the project file has no `NativeBiometricVault` references at all. Without target membership, `RCTNativeBiometricVault.mm` will not be compiled into the app binary, so `NativeBiometricVault` can never be registered at runtime even if CI build-ios is green.

`codeForOSStatus()` maps `errSecAuthFailed` to `AUTH_FAILED` unconditionally at lines 99-100. For a Keychain item protected with `kSecAccessControlBiometryCurrentSet`, biometric enrollment changes can surface through `errSecAuthFailed` as part of the invalidated-key path; the validation contract calls out that this case must surface as `KEY_INVALIDATED` so the JS restore flow can trigger. As written, some enrollment-change failures will be misclassified as an auth retry instead of forcing recovery.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance does not currently spell out that `ios/EnboxMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` still uses explicit source-file membership rather than Xcode 16 file-system-synced groups. That omission made it easy for the worker to assume 'no Xcode project changes required' and miss target registration for the new Obj-C++ file.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `ios/EnboxMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:242-249` shows `PBXSourcesBuildPhase` listing only `AppDelegate.swift in Sources`, and a search for `NativeBiometricVault` in that project file returns no matches. The handoff for session `330168f5-79cf-4ba9-9a91-46815887a146` states that RN 0.85 required no Xcode project changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mobile-feature-worker` procedure requires workers to load `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, but the reviewed transcript skeleton skipped those reads and never verified `project.pbxproj` target membership. Tightening the skill or its checklist around iOS target-registration proof would make this class of omission less likely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:20-22` requires those three library reads. The `worker-transcripts.jsonl` entry for session `330168f5-79cf-4ba9-9a91-46815887a146` shows reads of `mission.md`, `validation-contract.md`, `AGENTS.md`, the native files, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, and `.factory/services.yaml`, but no reads of the required library docs and no inspection of `ios/EnboxMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`.

summary: Fail: I found two blocking issues in the iOS biometric vault feature — the new `RCTNativeBiometricVault.mm` file was never added to the Xcode target, and `errSecAuthFailed` is still mapped to `AUTH_FAILED` instead of the required `KEY_INVALIDATED` invalidation path. I also noted a shared-state gap around undocumented iOS target-membership conventions and a skill-procedure gap in the reviewed worker transcript.
